characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: A college student who works as a caretaker for the children of the Pembroke family, and later the Powell family. Big Brother Mentor: Charles can be seen as an older mentor for both the Pembroke and Powell children. Butt-Monkey: He often gets into awkward situations when he has to deal with the kids, Buddy, and other characters at the same time. Especially in later seasons, since the Powell kids (exceptSarah) are brattier towards him than the Pembrokes. Catchphrase: "You had to (something that results in some sort of foul-up), didn't ya?!", which is more often said to Buddy but occasionally said to other characters. Chick Magnet: He attracts beautiful female characters all the time, sometimes unintentionally.

characters.name: Charles
characters.description: A college student who works as a caretaker for the children of the Pembroke family, and later the Powell family. Big Brother Mentor: Charles can be seen as an older mentor for both the Pembroke and Powell children. Butt-Monkey: He often gets into awkward situations when he has to deal with the kids, Buddy, and other characters at the same time. Especially in later seasons, since the Powell kids (exceptSarah) are brattier towards him than the Pembrokes. Catchphrase: "You had to (something that results in some sort of foul-up), didn't ya?!", which is more often said to Buddy but occasionally said to other characters. Chick Magnet: He attracts beautiful female characters all the time, sometimes unintentionally.

characters.name: Buddy Lembeck
characters.description: Charles' best friend. And Starring: In the first season, he is credited as "and Willie Aames" at the end. From season 2 onwards, he is credited second after Scott Baio (Charles). Casanova Wannabe: Zig-Zagged. He likes to think he'sThe Casanova, but he is hit and miss in this department. He does have a few girlfriends in the series but is more often turned down and/or slapped by females he comes on to. Characterization Marches On: In season 1, he is just immature and obsessed with girls, but not particularly stupid. He becomes much dumber in the Powell years, especially from season 3 onwards. Cloudcuckoolander: Increasingly so as the series goes on. By later seasons, he says something completely nonsensical in every episode.

characters.name: Stan Pembroke
characters.description: The patriarch of the Pembroke family. Happily Married: With Jill. Sometimes Charles looks after the kids when their parents want to spend time together. Parents as People: Downplayed. He is not a bad father, but he often expects Charles to solve all the problems with the kids.

characters.name: Jill Pembroke
characters.description: The matriarch of the Pembroke family. Happily Married: With Stan. Sometimes Charles looks after the kids when their parents want to spend time together.

characters.name: Lila Pembroke
characters.description: The eldest of the Pembroke siblings. Bratty Teenage Daughter: A quite realistic girl with teenage issues, and she can be snippy and argumentative. Decomposite Character: In the following seasons, Lila's traits are later divided into the two Powell teenage daughters: Lila can be an insecure and awkwardNaïve Everygirl(like Sarah), but is also a boy-crazyBratty Teenage Daughter(like Jamie). Hormone-Addled Teenager: Lila is shown to be boy-crazy, especially when it comes to cool boys.

characters.name: Douglas Pembroke
characters.description: The middle Pembroke sibling. Cloudcuckoolander: He lives in his own world, and even his siblings find his weird. Stereotypical Nerd: A geeky kid with glasses who wears alien masks, is a fan of science-fiction, and he likesDungeons & Dragons.

characters.name: Jason Pembroke
characters.description: The youngest of the Pembroke siblings. Adorably Precocious Child: A cute kid who is very sassy and witty, despite his young age. Deadpan Snarker: He tends to make snarky remarks towards his weird brother Douglas. Intergenerational Friendship: Of the Pembroke kids, he is the youngest and probably the closest to his "nanny" Charles.

characters.name: Walter Powell
characters.description: The grandfather of the Powell siblings. And Starring: "and James Callahan as Walter Powell" Brutal Honesty: He tends to have blunt one-liners towards both Charles and Buddy. Dad the Veteran: A grandfather who was a war veteran, and his son is currently a Navy commander. Grumpy Old Man: He is an old man and the grumpiest person in the house.

characters.name: Ellen Powell
characters.description: Advertised Extra: Even though Sandra Kerns is still credited as a main cast member in the opening credits, she only appears once in Season 4 and twice in Season 5. Hands-Off Parenting: Starting with season 4, she falls into this category, as her portrayer Sandra Kerns appears in substantially few episodes during the fourth and fifth seasons.

characters.name: Jamie Powell
characters.description: The eldest of the Powell siblings. Always Someone Better: To her younger sister Sarah. Jamie is more popular, and Sarah thinks everyone likes Jamie better. Bratty Teenage Daughter: A superficial and selfish girl who always thinks about her looks and clothes. Childish Older Sibling: She is less mature than the smart middle daughter Sarah. Hormone-Addled Teenager: Jamie is this to some degree, in terms of her love interests. She is rather boy-crazy.

characters.name: Sarah Powell
characters.description: The middle Powell sibling. I Am Not Pretty: She considers herself less pretty than her popular sister Jamie. Big Little Sister: She is taller than Jamie, despite being younger. Bookworm: Known for being the bookish and studious sister inThe Glorious War of Sisterly Rivalrywith Jamie. Middle Child Syndrome: The middle child of the Powells, and also the most shy and insecure.

characters.name: Adam Powell
characters.description: The youngest of the Powell siblings. Bratty Half-Pint: Like his older sister Jamie, he can be a brat towards Charles. Suspiciously Similar Substitute: To Jason Pembroke from the first season. A rambunctious little boy and the youngest in the family.
